citeinfo.title: Aerial Fire Retardant Avoidance Areas - Terrestrial

descript.abstract: This data depicts terrestrial aerial fire retardant avoidance areas delivered as part of the 2011 Nationwide Aerial Application of Fire Retardant on National Forest System Land Environmental Impact Statement, the 2023 Supplemental Environmental Impact Statement, and the 2024 Record of Decision. This data shows areas, provided by each National Forest, where the aerial application of fire retardant should be avoided in order to prevent the potential of impacts to Federally listed threatened or endangered species as identified through consultation, or Forest Service sensitive species.This data is to be used in planning and implementation phases of U.S. Forest Service fire activities to help prevent intrusions of aerial fire retardant in known areas of TEPCS (Threatened, Endangered, Proposed, Candidate, Sensitive) species throughout National Forest lands. Provided here is a National merged dataset derived from each National Forest contribution. This data has been merged, dissolved, and erased of attributes contained in each original component dataset. For this purpose, specific attributes are not necessary, as any spatial areas depicted simply show areas where aerial fire retardant use is to be avoided as stated in U.S. Forest Service guidelines.
descript.purpose: This data depicts terrestrial aerial fire retardant avoidance areas for use in digital, hardcopy, and online mapping to support the 2011 Record of Decision for the Nationwide Aerial Application of Fire Retardant on National Forest System Land EIS, the 2023 Supplemental Environmental Impact Statement, and the 2024 Record of Decision. This data should be used in conjunction with aquatic aerial fire retardant avoidance area data. An aerial fire retardant avoidance area is defined as an area in which application of aerial fire retardant is prohibited to avoid, limit, or mitigate potential impacts to specified resources. For additional information please see the Interagency Wildland Fire Chemicals Policy and Guidance: https:/www.fs.usda.gov/managing-land/fire/chemicals
descript.supplinf: This dataset is an aggregation of areas where the application of aerial fire retardant may impact one or more aquatic or terrestrial Endangered Species Act threatened, endangered, proposed, candidate, or Regional Forester sensitive plant or animal species (TEPCS), or designated critical habitat, as identified in consultation. Annually, a formal request for updates to this dataset is sent to all national forest system units. However, updates can be made at any time throughout the year. An automated process captures any updates to the data and aggregates it into this national dataset in the Enterprise Data Warehouse daily. A Unit ID attribute specifies the region and forest of the feature’s location.

timeperd.current: National spatial extent based on data represented on the update date.

idinfo.useconst: The USDA Forest Service makes no warranty, expressed or implied, including the warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose, nor assumes any legal liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness or utility of these geospatial data, or for the improper or incorrect use of these geospatial data. These geospatial data and related maps or graphics are not legal documents and are not intended to be used as such. The data and maps may not be used to determine title, ownership, legal descriptions or boundaries, legal jurisdiction, or restrictions that may be in place on either public or private land. Natural hazards may or may not be depicted on the data and maps, and users should exercise due caution. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible to verify the limitations of the geospatial data and to use the data accordingly.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

cntinfo.cntvoice: Please send an e-mail to the address below.

dataqual.logic: Generally the source information used to record spatial locations are from 1) on-screen digitizing with a georeferenced topographic or image background; or 2) GPS field collected positions.
dataqual.complete: Data is complete as of publication

horizpa.horizpar: Generally the source information used to record spatial locations are from: 1) on-screen digitizing with a georeferenced topographic or image background; or 2) GPS field collected positions.

procstep.procdesc: Avoidance data adjustments related to Endangered Species Act threatened, endangered, proposed, and candidate species must be coordinated with the United States Department of the Interior Fish and Wildlife Service (Fish and Wildlife Service) and the United States Department of Commerce National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration National Marine Fisheries Service (NOAA Fisheries). Avoidance areas for listed species are determined through Endangered Species Act Section 7 consultations that occur at a national level, using information provided by Forest Service Regional Offices and National Forest System forests and grasslands. Changes and updates rely on information provided by these National Forest System units, as well as on changes in listed species status or changes resulting from nationwide consultation updates or reinitiations. The following actions occur annually to ensure that any updates have been considered, reviewed, and implemented at all levels: Annual coordination with the Fish and Wildlife Service and NOAA Fisheries will occur to review changes in federal listing status, completed consultations, and other information and to determine any changes that may be needed to avoidance areas for listed species. Most changes to avoidance areas for listed species should occur at the time the Washington Office makes the annual request for data updates. However, updates to avoidance areas for listed species can occur at any time if needed as a result of changes in federal listing status, completed Endangered Species Act consultations, or revisions agreed on after interagency meetings. If updates are required outside of the annual update timeframe, the new avoidance area data will be automatically updated in the Enterprise Data Warehouse and other data sources, however the static pdf maps will not reflect the changes. Changes to avoidance areas for listed species must not create adverse effects that are greater than those analyzed in the relevant Biological Assessments or that would change determinations made in the relevant Biological Opinions. Changes to avoidance areas proposed by National Forest System units, other than updates based on Endangered Species Act consultation requirements or changes in listed species status or distribution, must be approved in writing by the Forest Supervisor. National Forest System units should use critical habitat layers and species ranges, population, or site data designated by the Fish and Wildlife Service and NOAA Fisheries, when available, to create avoidance area geospatial data files. Each species requiring avoidance areas should have its own avoidance area(s) even if avoidance areas for one species overlap those of another, or overlap aquatic avoidance areas or their buffers. All data is aggregated and assigned a Unit ID representing the region and forest numbers. Updates can be made at any time throughout the year by forest system units. An automated process captures any updates to the data and aggregates it into this national dataset in the Enterprise Data Warehouse daily.

attr.attrdef: The area of the feature in acres, calculated using a projected surface computation. A NAD 83, USA Contiguous Albers Equal Area Conic projection was used for Regions 1-9, and NAD 83, Alaska Albers for Region 10.

attrdomv.udom: The area of the feature in acres, calculated using a projected surface computation. A NAD 83, USA Contiguous Albers Equal Area Conic projection was used for Regions 1-9, and NAD 83, Alaska Albers for Region 10.

attr.attrdef: The Unit ID is the administrative unit identifier for the National Forest managing each feature. The first two characters are the Region identifier and the second two characters are the Forest identifier. This results in a unique identifier for each National Forest.

edom.edomvd: GREEN MOUNTAIN AND FINGER LAKES NATIONAL FORESTS
FRANCIS MARION AND SUMTER NATIONAL FORESTS
COLUMBIA RIVER GORGE NATIONAL SCENIC AREA
PIKE AND SAN ISABEL NATIONAL FORESTS
